rulings.Case Title: GAIL BARRETT VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, APRIL 6, 2007, LINE 11 RESPONDENT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, SAN FRANCISCO, SAN FRANCISCO POLICE DEPARTMENT'S DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION. 1) PETITION IS SUBJECT TO THE CITY'S RETIREMENT SYSTEM AND THE RULES AND CASES APPLICABLE TO PERL AND CERL ARE NOT A BASIS FOR GRANTING THE WRIT. KRAFT V. CITY OF OAKLAND (1999) 68 CAL.APP. 4TH 46. 2) THE CITY HAS NO OBLIGATION TO REFER PETITONER'S CLAIM TO WCAB. GEOGHEHAN V, RETIREMENT BOARD OF CCSF (1990) 22 CAL.APP.3RD 1525, 1533. THE RETIREMENT BOARD IS THE SOLE AUTHORITY FOR DETERMINING BENEFITS UNDER THE CITY'S RETIREMENT SYSTEM. CHARTERS 12.100, 12,102. 3) THE RETIREMENT BOARD RULE 5.06 DOES NOT REQUIRE A REHEARING AND THE DECISION NOT TO REHEAR APPEARS TO BE NEITHER ARBITRARY NOR CAPRICIOUS. 4) PETITIONER EXHAUSTED HER DISABILITY LEAVE. THE RETIREMENT BOARD PROVISION RELATED TO REINSTATEMENT - FORMER RULE 5.05, NOW POLICY PARAGRAPH 26 - CHARTER SECTION A8, 568-3 AND APPLY ONLY TO EMPLYEES FOUND TO BE ENTITLED TO A DISABILITY RETIREMENT. PETITIONER HAS BEEN FOUND NOT TO BE ENTITLED TO THIS BENEFIT. 5) PETITIONER'S CLAIM FOR DISABILITY APPEARS TO RELATE EXCLUSIVELY TO INJURIES THAT WERE SUSTAINED BEFORE THE JANUARY 7, 2002 HEARING. GIVEN THAT PETITIONER SEEKS A PERMANENT DISABILITY DETERMINATION, RES JUDICATA APPEARS TO BAR THE PETITION. EICHMAN V. FOTOMAT CORP. (1983) 147 CAL.APP. 3RD 1170,1174; TAKAHASHI V. BOARD OF EDUCATION (1988) 202 CAL.APP. 3RD 1464. FOR THE REASONS STATED, IT DOES NOT APPEAR THAT PETITIONER HAS A BASIS FOR AMENDING THE PETITION. AS A CONSEQUENCE, THE DEMURRER TO THE SECOND AMENDED PETITION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. =(302/PJM/JU)
